title: Why combine data from multiple sources?

blocks.content:
Most infrastructure doesn't run on a single backend. Metrics might live in Prometheus, business data in PostgreSQL, and logs in Loki. Each data source has its own query language and its own view of your systems.

When you build a dashboard, each panel typically queries one data source at a time. That works well for focused views, but it falls short when you need to see the full picture. For example:

- **Correlate metrics with business data** — CPU usage from Prometheus alongside order counts from PostgreSQL.
- **Unify monitoring across environments** — Metrics from two separate Prometheus instances in one chart.
- **Enrich time series with context** — Logs from Loki matched to Prometheus alerts by timestamp.

## What you'll do in this path

Grafana's Mixed data source lets you query multiple backends in a single panel. Combined with transformations like Join by field, you can merge the results into a unified visualization.

In this path, you'll use the built-in Grafana (TestData) data source to simulate querying two different backends. The workflow is identical to combining real data sources like Prometheus and PostgreSQL.

In the next milestone, you create a panel and select the Mixed data source.
